title: 1.1: States that memory is allocated in segments of approximately 5 MB per 2048 lines, with segment size depending on terminal column width
1.2: Reports RSS growth measurements at segment allocation boundaries showing measurable memory jumps (e.g., ~4.75 MB jumps at lines 2048, 4096, 6144)
1.3: Reports total memory growth summary comparing baseline RSS to final RSS after buffer operations (e.g., growth from ~17 MB baseline to ~167 MB final)
1.4: States that segments are allocated on demand when lines are pushed, not when the buffer is initially created with a given capacity
1.5: Reports scroll operation timing measurements showing O(1) complexity (e.g., ~0.06-0.1 µs consistently whether at scroll position 0, 1000, or 9000)
1.6: States that scroll position remains stable when new output arrives while viewing history, with the view maintaining its relative position rather than jumping
1.7: Reports line access timing showing constant access time across different scroll positions (e.g., 60-80 nanoseconds whether at position 0, 1000, or 9000)

1.8: States that the ring buffer wraps around when line count reaches the configured scrollback limit, with oldest lines being overwritten circularly and count staying at the maximum capacity (e.g., count remains at 6144 after line 6145 is pushed)
1.10: Reports output generation rate measurement under load conditions (e.g., ~300,000+ lines per second throughput)
